(f), (g). , §§ 107(c), 207(2), temporarily added subsecs. (f) and (g). See Effective and Termination Dates of 2003 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 108–771996—Subsec. (c)(1). inserted “, filing of a reconciliation,” after “entry”.Pub. L. 104–2951993—Subsec. (a). , amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) read as follows: “Any owner, importer, consignee, or agent thereof who imports, or who knowingly causes to be imported, any merchandise into the customs territory of the United States shall make, keep, and render for examination and inspection such records (including statements, declarations, and other documents) which—Pub. L. 103–182, § 614(1)“(1) pertain to any such importation, or to the information contained in the documents required by this chapter in connection with the entry of merchandise; and“(2) are normally kept in the ordinary course of business.”Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “Any person who exports, or who knowingly causes to be exported, any merchandise to Canada shall make, keep, and render for examination and inspection such records (including certifications of origin or copies thereof) which pertain to such exportations.”Pub. L. 103–182, § 205(a)(1)Subsec. (c). , amended generally subsec. (c), as amended by (see below). Prior to amendment, subsec. (c) read as follows: “The records required by subsections (a) and (b) of this section shall be kept for such period of time, not to exceed 5 years from the date of entry or exportation, as appropriate, as the Secretary shall prescribe; except that records for any drawback claim shall be kept until the 3rd anniversary of the date of payment of the claim.” See Construction of 1993 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 103–182, § 205(a)(2)Pub. L. 103–182, § 614(2), amended subsec. (c) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (c) read as follows: “The records required by subsection (a) and (b) of this section shall be kept for such periods of time, not to exceed 5 years from the date of entry, as the Secretary shall prescribe.” See Construction of 1993 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 103–182, § 614(2)Subsec. (e). , amended subsec. (e) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (e) read as follows: “Any person who fails to retain records required by subsection (b) of this section or the regulations issued to implement that subsection shall be liable to a civil penalty not to exceed $10,000.”Pub. L. 103–182, § 205(a)(3)1988—Subsecs. (b) to (e). temporarily added subsec. (b), redesignated former subsec. (b) as (c) and inserted “and (b)” after “subsection (a)”, redesignated former subsec. (c) as (d), and added subsec. (e). See Effective and Termination Dates of 1988 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 100–449

Effective Date of 2020 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 116–260section 601(h) of div. O of Pub. L. 116–260section 81c of this titleJuly 1, 2020, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–113, title II, § 206(b)134 Stat. 48Jan. 29, 2020“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall take effect on the date on which the USMCA enters into force [].July 1, 2020“(2)Applicability.—“(A).—ExportsParagraphs (2) and (5)(A) of section 508(b) of the Tariff Act of 1930 [, (5)(A)], as amended by subsection (a), shall apply with respect to a good exported from the United States on or after the date on which the USMCA enters into force.19 U.S.C. 1508(b)(2)“(B).—ImportsParagraphs (4) and (5)(C) of section 508(b) of the Tariff Act of 1930 [, (5)(C)], as amended by subsection (a), shall apply with respect to a good that is entered for consumption, or withdrawn from warehouse for consumption, on or after the date on which the USMCA enters into force.19 U.S.C. 1508(b)(4)“(3)Transition from nafta treatment.—“(A).—ExportsIn the case of a good exported from the United States before the date on which the USMCA enters into force—“(i)the amendments made by subsection (a) to paragraphs (2) and (5)(A) of section 508(b) of the Tariff Act of 1930 () shall not apply with respect to the good; and19 U.S.C. 1508“(ii)section 508 of such Act, as in effect on the day before that date, shall continue to apply on and after that date with respect to the good.“(B).—ImportsIn the case of a good that is entered for consumption, or withdrawn from warehouse for consumption, before the date on which the USMCA enters into force, the amendments made by subsection (a) to paragraphs (4) and (5)(C) of section 508(b) of the Tariff Act of 1930 () shall not apply with respect to the good.”19 U.S.C. 1508[For definition of “USMCA” as used in , set out above, see .]section 206(b) of Pub. children.label: A
children.text: imports merchandise into the customs territory of the United States, files a drawback claim, or transports or stores merchandise carried or held under bond, or

children.label: B
children.text: knowingly causes the importation or transportation or storage of merchandise carried or held under bond into or from the customs territory of the United States;
agent of any party described in paragraph (1); or
person whose activities require the filing of a declaration or entry, or both;

children.label: A
children.text: pertain to any such activity, or to the information contained in the records required by this chapter in connection with any such activity; and

children.label: B
children.text: are normally kept in the ordinary course of business.

children.heading: Exports and imports relating to USMCA countries
children.label: b Exports and imports relating to USMCA countries

children.heading: USMCA; USMCA country
children.label: A USMCA; USMCA country
children.text: The terms “USMCA” and “USMCA country” have the meanings given those terms in .section 4502 of this title

children.heading: USMCA certification of origin
children.label: B USMCA certification of origin
children.text: The term “USMCA certification of origin” means the certification established under article 5.2.1 of the USMCA that a good qualifies as an originating good under the USMCA.

children.label: A
children.text: the purchase, cost, value, and shipping of, and payment for, the good;

children.label: B
children.text: the purchase, cost, value, and shipping of, and payment for, all materials, including indirect materials, used in the production of the good; and

children.label: C
children.text: the production of the good in the form in which it was exported or the production of the material in the form in which it was sold.

children.heading: Exports under the Canadian Agreement
children.label: 3 Exports under the Canadian Agreement
children.text: Any person who exports, or who knowingly causes to be exported, any merchandise to Canada during such time as the United States-Canada Free-Trade Agreement is in force with respect to, and the United States applies that Agreement to, Canada shall make, keep, and render for examination and inspection such records (including certifications of origin or copies thereof) which pertain to the exportations.

children.label: i
children.text: records and supporting documentation related to the importation;

children.label: ii
children.text: all records and supporting documents related to the origin of the good (including the certification or copies thereof), if the importer completed the certification; and

children.label: iii
children.text: records and supporting documents necessary to demonstrate that the good did not, while in transit to the United States, undergo further production or any other operation other than unloading, reloading, or any other operation necessary to preserve the good in good condition or to transport the good to the United States.

children.heading: Vehicle producer
children.label: B Vehicle producer
children.text: Any vehicle producer whose good is the subject of a claim for preferential tariff treatment under the USMCA shall make, keep, and, pursuant to rules and regulations promulgated by the Secretary of the Treasury and Secretary of Labor, render for examination and inspection records and supporting documents related to the labor value content and steel and aluminum purchasing requirements for the qualification of its vehicles for preferential treatment.

children.heading: Exports to USMCA countries
children.label: A Exports to USMCA countries
children.text: A person covered by paragraph (2) who completes a USMCA certification of origin or provides a written representation for a good exported from the United States to a USMCA country shall keep the records required by such paragraph relating to that certification of origin for a period of at least 5 years after the date on which the certification is completed.

children.label: i
children.text: no period of time for the retention of the records may exceed 5 years from the date of entry, filing of a reconciliation, or exportation, as appropriate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: records for any drawback claim shall be kept until the third anniversary of the date of liquidation of the claim.

children.heading: Imports into the United States
children.label: C Imports into the United States

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An importer covered by paragraph (4)(A) shall keep the records and supporting documents required by such paragraph for a period of at least 5 years after the date of importation of the good.

children.heading: Vehicle producer
children.label: ii Vehicle producer
children.text: A vehicle producer covered by paragraph (4)(B) shall keep the records and supporting documents required by paragraph (4)(B) for a period of at least 5 years after the date of filing the certifications required under paragraphs (1) and (2) of .section 4532(c) of this title
no period of time for the retention of the records required under subsection (a) may exceed 5 years from the date of entry, filing of a reconciliation, or exportation, as appropriate; and
records for any drawback claim shall be kept until the 3rd anniversary of the date of liquidation of the claim.
the terms and conditions of the importation are controlled by the person placing the order; or

technical data, molds, equipment, other production assistance, material, components, or parts are furnished by the person placing the order with knowledge that they will be used in the manufacture or production of the imported merchandise.

children.label: A
children.text: a civil penalty not to exceed $10,000; or

children.label: B
children.text: the general recordkeeping penalty that applies under the customs laws;

children.heading: Relating to Canadian agreement exports
children.label: 2 Relating to Canadian agreement exports
children.text: Any person who fails to retain the records required by paragraph (3) of subsection (b) or the regulations issued to implement that paragraph shall be liable for a civil penalty not to exceed $10,000.

children.heading: Certificates of Origin for goods exported under the United States-Chile Free Trade Agreement
children.label: f Certificates of Origin for goods exported under the United States-Chile Free Trade Agreement
i
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, the good;

children.label: ii
children.text: if applicable, the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, all materials, including recovered goods, used in the production of the good; and

children.label: iii
children.text: if applicable, the production of the good in the form in which it was exported.

children.heading: Chile FTA Certificate of Origin
children.label: B Chile FTA Certificate of Origin
children.text: The term “Chile FTA Certificate of Origin” means the certification, established under article 4.13 of the United States-Chile Free Trade Agreement, that a good qualifies as an originating good under such Agreement.

children.heading: Exports to Chile
children.label: 2 Exports to Chile
children.text: Any person who completes and issues a Chile FTA Certificate of Origin for a good exported from the United States shall make, keep, and, pursuant to rules and regulations promulgated by the Secretary of the Treasury, render for examination and inspection all records and supporting documents related to the origin of the good (including the Certificate or copies thereof).

children.heading: Retention period
children.label: 3 Retention period
children.text: Records and supporting documents shall be kept by the person who issued a Chile FTA Certificate of Origin for at least 5 years after the date on which the certificate was issued.

children.heading: Certifications of origin for goods exported under the Dominican Republic-Central America-United States Free Trade Agreement
children.label: g Certifications of origin for goods exported under the Dominican Republic-Central America-United States Free Trade Agreement
i
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, the good;

children.label: ii
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, all materials, including indirect materials, used in the production of the good; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the production of the good in the form in which it was exported.

children.heading: CAFTA–DR certification of origin
children.label: B CAFTA–DR certification of origin
children.text: The term “CAFTA–DR certification of origin” means the certification established under article 4.16 of the Dominican Republic-Central America-United States Free Trade Agreement that a good qualifies as an originating good under such Agreement.

children.heading: Exports to CAFTA–DR countries
children.label: 2 Exports to CAFTA–DR countries
children.text: Any person who completes and issues a CAFTA–DR certification of origin for a good exported from the United States shall make, keep, and, pursuant to rules and regulations promulgated by the Secretary of the Treasury, render for examination and inspection all records and supporting documents related to the origin of the good (including the certification or copies thereof).

children.heading: Retention period
children.label: 3 Retention period
children.text: Records and supporting documents shall be kept by the person who issued a CAFTA–DR certification of origin for at least 5 years after the date on which the certification was issued.

children.heading: Certifications of origin for goods exported under the United States-Peru Trade Promotion Agreement
children.label: h Certifications of origin for goods exported under the United States-Peru Trade Promotion Agreement
i
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, the good;

children.label: ii
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, all materials, including indirect materials, used in the production of the good; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the production of the good in the form in which it was exported.

children.heading: PTPA certification of origin
children.label: B PTPA certification of origin
children.text: The term “PTPA certification of origin” means the certification established under article 4.15 of the United States-Peru Trade Promotion Agreement that a good qualifies as an originating good under such Agreement.

children.heading: Exports to Peru
children.label: 2 Exports to Peru
children.text: Any person who completes and issues a PTPA certification of origin for a good exported from the United States shall make, keep, and, pursuant to rules and regulations promulgated by the Secretary of the Treasury, render for examination and inspection all records and supporting documents related to the origin of the good (including the certification or copies thereof).

children.heading: Retention period
children.label: 3 Retention period
children.text: The person who issues a PTPA certification of origin shall keep the records and supporting documents relating to that certification of origin for a period of at least 5 years after the date on which the certification is issued.

children.heading: Certifications of origin for goods exported under the United States–Korea Free Trade Agreement
children.label: i Certifications of origin for goods exported under the United States–Korea Free Trade Agreement
i
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, the good;

children.label: ii
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, all materials, including indirect materials, used in the production of the good; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the production of the good in the form in which it was exported.

children.heading: KFTA certification of origin
children.label: B KFTA certification of origin
children.text: The term “KFTA certification of origin” means the certification established under article 6.15 of the United States–Korea Free Trade Agreement that a good qualifies as an originating good under such Agreement.

children.heading: Exports to Korea
children.label: 2 Exports to Korea
children.text: Any person who completes and issues a KFTA certification of origin for a good exported from the United States shall make, keep, and, pursuant to rules and regulations promulgated by the Secretary of the Treasury, render for examination and inspection all records and supporting documents related to the origin of the good (including the certification or copies thereof).

children.heading: Retention period
children.label: 3 Retention period
children.text: The person who issues a KFTA certification of origin shall keep the records and supporting documents relating to that certification of origin for a period of at least 5 years after the date on which the certification is issued.

children.heading: Certifications of origin for goods exported under the United States–Colombia Trade Promotion Agreement
children.label: j Certifications of origin for goods exported under the United States–Colombia Trade Promotion Agreement
i
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, the good;

children.label: ii
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, all materials, including indirect materials, used in the production of the good; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the production of the good in the form in which it was exported.

children.heading: CTPA certification of origin
children.label: B CTPA certification of origin
children.text: The term “CTPA certification of origin” means the certification established under article 4.15 of the United States–Colombia Trade Promotion Agreement that a good qualifies as an originating good under such Agreement.

children.heading: Exports to Colombia
children.label: 2 Exports to Colombia
children.text: Any person who completes and issues a CTPA certification of origin for a good exported from the United States shall make, keep, and, pursuant to rules and regulations promulgated by the Secretary of the Treasury, render for examination and inspection all records and supporting documents related to the origin of the good (including the certification or copies thereof).

children.heading: Retention period
children.label: 3 Retention period
children.text: The person who issues a CTPA certification of origin shall keep the records and supporting documents relating to that certification of origin for a period of at least 5 years after the date on which the certification is issued.

children.heading: Certifications of origin for goods exported under the United States–Panama Trade Promotion Agreement
children.label: k Certifications of origin for goods exported under the United States–Panama Trade Promotion Agreement
i
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, the good;

children.label: ii
children.text: the purchase, cost, and value of, and payment for, all materials, including indirect materials, used in the production of the good; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the production of the good in the form in which it was exported.

children.heading: Panama TPA certification of origin
children.label: B Panama TPA certification of origin
children.text: The term “Panama TPA certification of origin” means the certification established under article 4.15 of the United States–Panama Trade Promotion Agreement that a good qualifies as an originating good under such Agreement.

children.heading: Exports to Panama
children.label: 2 Exports to Panama
children.text: Any person who completes and issues a Panama TPA certification of origin for a good exported from the United States shall make, keep, and, pursuant to rules and regulations promulgated by the Secretary of the Treasury, render for examination and inspection all records and supporting documents related to the origin of the good (including the certification or copies thereof).

children.heading: Retention period
children.label: 3 Retention period
children.text: The person who issues a Panama TPA certification of origin shall keep the records and supporting documents relating to that certification of origin for a period of at least 5 years after the date on which the certification is issued.
a civil penalty not to exceed $10,000; or
the general record keeping penalty that applies under the customs laws of the United States.
